summary: Justin Brennan provides a 2025 market update and detailed walkthrough of multifamily acquisition mechanics. Market conditions show sticky interest rates (10-year treasury at 4.6%, mortgage rates in high sixes, commercial loans in low sixes to low sevens), declining property values due to rising cap rates, and distressed deals from variable-rate debt failures. He teaches the Five Pillars framework: finding deals (via brokers to access pre-market inventory), analyzing (using NOI and key metrics like 15-20% IRR, 6-10% CoC, 1.8x+ equity multiple), financing (70% leverage max, general partner brings 10% equity), closing, and managing properties.
takeaways: Find deals through on-market brokers to access pre-market inventory (60% of deals never listed publicly)
Analyze deals requiring all three metrics: 15-20% IRR, 6-10% cash-on-cash return, 1.8x+ equity multiple—not just one or two
Structure as general partner with 10% of down payment while raising 90% from limited partners; get 30-40% deal ownership plus acquisition fee (1-3% of purchase price)
On $1M deal needing $300K down: GP brings $30K, gets $20-30K acquisition fee at close, leaving ~$0-10K true out-of-pocket
Every $1,000/month NOI increase = ~$220,000 property value increase (based on cap rate formula)
Target properties: 10-50 units in middle U.S. markets (GA/TN/AL included); avoid over-leveraging beyond 75% LTV
Use 70% loan-to-value financing maximum; residential financing available for 10-27 unit buildings with minimal experience if property size is 'bite-sizable'

skill_candidates.change: Implement three-metric underwriting filter (15-20% IRR, 6-10% CoC, 1.8x equity multiple) to eliminate non-qualifying deals faster
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explicitly states 'if it meets all of those metrics all of them not like two not one...it's a good deal give or take' and emphasizes this baseline separates good from bad deals

skill_candidates.change: Build broker relationships in target markets by touring 3+ on-market properties per visit to gain pre-market deal access within 2-3 weeks
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin walked through his exact process: find 3 brokers on LoopNet, call with specific property questions, schedule site visits, then ask for pre-market inventory; he now receives 45 pre-market emails daily using this method

skill_candidates.change: Create soft commitments from personal network (Trader Joe's checkout example) before sourcing deals, building investor CRM with target check sizes ($25K-250K range)
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrates soft commitment process: casual conversation → connection → interest stated → added to CRM; states 'money will chase deals' if deal metrics are solid, making investor-first approach inefficient

title: What To REALLY Expect From The 2025 Market Update
